summary: A brief biographical overview of Plato, highlighting his founding of the Academy, his authorship of the Republic, and his metaphysical theory of forms. It notes his emphasis on mathematics and his wariness of public life following the execution of his teacher, Socrates.

principles.statement: A wise society is run by a philosopher.
principles.rationale: Plato detailed this vision of governance and societal structure in his most famous work, the Republic.

principles.statement: Science and mathematics are of fundamental importance to education.
principles.rationale: He stressed the importance of these subjects at the Academy, which led to him being known as the 'maker of mathematicians.'

mental_models.name: Theory of Forms
mental_models.description: A metaphysical theory showcased throughout Plato's early, middle, and late dialogues.

anti_patterns.why_it_fails: The unjust treatment of his teacher, Socrates, by both oligarchic and democratic systems demonstrated the dangers and unreliability of participating in public politics.
